title: Herdessan Guild
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Government |image= |name=Herdessan Guild |type= |founding= |constitution= |headofstate= |headofgov= |commander= |executive= |legislative= |judicial= |military= |sector= |system= |celestialbody=[[Herdessa/Legends|Herdessa]][[Star Wars (1977) 88|''Star Wars'' (1977) 88]] |capital= |language= |currency= |religious= |holiday= |anthem= |formed= |established= |fragmented=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|''My'' voice is the people's voice. My guild and I always decided what is good for all, and..."
"What Brylin means is that our people are happy with our leadership... they made their wishes known when they chose us to rule them. They will accept our choices now.|Brylin and [[Tof (Human)|Tof]]|}} The '''Herdessan Guild''' was the organization that ruled the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Herdessa/Legends|Herdessa]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Known for cruelty, excess, and for exploiting the planet's lower classes, the Herdessan Guild instituted a lucrative [[Slavery/Legends|slaving]] operation in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], unwittingly playing a role in a [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] tactic to spread unrest and chaos in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] ahead of a planned [[Nagai–Tof War|invasion]]. However, [[Alliance of Free Planets]] leader [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], along with a popular resistance movement, [[Battle of Herdessa (Nagai–Tof War)|overthrew]] the Herdessan Guild and ended their slaving activities. ==Description== {{Quote|I've never seen any group of planetary leaders so eager to please guests as Brylin and his guild appear to be.|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|}} The Herdessan Guild was the ruling body that governed the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Herdessa/Legends|Herdessa]], a world located along the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Originally chosen by the Herdessan people to rule over the planet, the Herdessan Guild later projected power over the lower classes through a bustling [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] trade established during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} The Herdessan Guild of the day enthusiastically participated in the practice, and shamelessly used their slaves as a way to ingratiate themselves with visiting diplomats. Among the common people of Herdessa, the Guild developed a reputation for excess and exploitation, and their security force was from time to time loosed upon rebellious elements in violent raids. ==History== {{Quote|On Herdessa, you learn young, unless you're a part of the Guild, to trust no one you don't have to, and to help yourself.|Suzu|}} [[File:Brylin.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Brylin, final Spokesman of the Herdessan Guild.]] In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Herdessan Guild became a small part of the buildup of the [[Nagai–Tof War]], when the obscure [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the dwarf [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] [[Firefist]]''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' attempted to invade [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] amidst the instability that followed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] crushing [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. The Nagai dispatched [[Lumiya]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] who had formerly worked for the Empire as a double agent within the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], to Herdessa as part of a strategy to sow chaos before their eventual attack. Lumiya greatly earned the trust of the Herdessan Guild, who named her chief of security and aided the Guild in setting up a lucrative slaving operation that counted remnants of the Empire as a major client. The Herdessan Guild's reputation for exploitation helped inspire a rebellion among the planet's lower classes, led by a young woman named [[Suzu]]. Later in 4 ABY, the Herdessan Guild—headed at the time by Spokesman [[Brylin]]—welcomed a delegation from the newly formed [[Alliance of Free Planets]], who traveled to Herdessa in order to establish official diplomatic relations. However, Brylin and his inner circle instead planned to take the Alliance diplomats hostage and sell them to the Empire, and to that end captured top Alliance leader [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]], among others. Delegation member [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] escaped the Guild's operation and linked up with Suzu's revolution, raising a fighting force to [[Battle of Herdessa (Nagai–Tof War)|attack]] the Guild as they attempted to hand the captives over to the Empire. Their resistance sparked a popular uprising that overthrew the Herdessan Guild and put an end to the slave trade on Herdessa—however, Lumiya's involvement with the Nagai conspiracy remained unknown. ==Behind the scenes== The Herdessan Guild appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 88|''Star Wars'' (1977) 88]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]], illustrated by [[Tom Palmer]] and released in [[1984]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 88|''Star Wars'' (1977) 88]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 88}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|II|34|Herdessa}} *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Political factions]]
